D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

Cinema Log Curves — Log C · S-Log3 · V-Log

three log encodings give 18% mid-grey at three different code values — that one number is the curve's signature

A log curve assigns code value per stop of light rather than per linear step, fitting 14–18 stops of sensor dynamic range into a 10-bit container. Each vendor pins their curve at its own middle-grey: Log C (Alexa EI 800) at 39.1%, S-Log3 at 41.0%, V-Log at 42.3%. Knowing the middle-grey code value uniquely identifies the curve.

The plot shows code value vs scene-linear exposure (stops, with 18% = 0). Crosshairs mark the slider position. Watch where the three curves cross the 18% line — the vertical-difference between them at that point is what cinematographers tune for when matching cameras.
